title: argyreia collinsiae
intro: Argyreia collinsiae, also known as the Indian climbers or moon vine, is a perennial climber of the Convolvulaceae family. It has been used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Argyreia collinsiae include its ability to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin problems, as well as its use as a diuretic and to aid in digestion. The herbal preparations of Argyreia collinsiae include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be made by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water.
